text: Handling deadlines is an essential skill in both work and study. Good time management helps you break tasks into smaller steps and start early, avoiding last-minute stress. Prioritization is equally important; focus on urgent or important tasks first before moving to less critical ones. Scope management means clearly understanding what a task requires and resisting the urge to add extra features or details that weren't asked for. Finally, open communication with your team or manager is vital – if you foresee a delay, inform them promptly so they can adjust expectations. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines effectively while maintaining quality.
